# Feature: Public Beta Documentation Set

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-DOCS-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Produce a clean, well-structured, easy-to-navigate documentation set for Wave Client's public beta — covering both the **VS Code extension** and the **web app** — authored in portable Markdown under `docs/`, hosted on GitHub now and structured so a static-site generator (VitePress/MkDocs) can be layered on later with zero file moves. Includes an easy-to-find in-app **Documentation** icon (both apps) that opens the docs in the browser, slimmed purpose-only package READMEs, a consolidator root README, an architecture/design guide, beta release notes, and a cleanup of redundant docs.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (coding standards, architecture, testing, logging) — none captured in `.spec-lite/memory.md` (file absent). Conventions below are specific to this feature.

### Approach

- **Authoring format**: Plain GitHub-Flavored Markdown. Folder-based layout where each folder has a `README.md` landing page (GitHub auto-renders it). No build step, fully portable. Layout is SSG-ready: a VitePress `.vitepress/config.ts` or `mkdocs.yml` can be dropped on top later without moving files.
- **Cross-linking over repetition**: Shared topics (auth, environments, variables) are documented once and linked from feature docs (e.g., `requests.md` links to `auth.md` / `environments.md` rather than re-explaining them).
- **Screenshots**: This feature does **not** capture live screenshots. Every place a screenshot belongs gets a Markdown image reference with descriptive alt-text pointing at `docs/images/<name>.png`, and `docs/images/SCREENSHOTS.md` lists every expected image as a checklist for the maintainer to fill in later.
- **In-app link**: A single shared constant `WAVE_DOCS_URL` and a small shared `DocsLinkButton` component live in `packages/core`. The button renders an icon-only anchor (`<a href={WAVE_DOCS_URL} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">`). VS Code webviews open `https` anchor links in the system browser by default, so no adapter / `postMessage` plumbing is required — the same component works in web and VS Code. Both `ConfigPanel.tsx` files render it in the existing sidebar footer next to Settings / theme toggle / BETA badge.
- **Source-of-truth for content**: `.spec-lite/feature-summary.md` (current feature state) is the primary content reference; existing READMEs and `docs/adapter-guide.md` are secondary references. `.spec-lite/TODO.md` is the source for the "future peek" / roadmap sections (curated, not exhaustive).
- **Dev/contributor content**: Dropped from public-facing READMEs for the beta. Existing buried contributor docs (`packages/web/scripts/START_DEV_README.md`, `packages/*/src/test/README.md`) are kept in place (not deleted) since their content isn't reproduced elsewhere; a TODO tracks consolidating them into a future `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

`.spec-lite/memory.md` does not exist, so there are no standing rules to override. **Task sub-item adaptation**: because this is a documentation feature, the standard `Unit Tests` sub-item is replaced by a **Link & structure check** for markdown-only tasks (verify internal links resolve, heading levels nest correctly, and screenshot placeholders are registered in `SCREENSHOTS.md`). Real `Unit Tests` apply only to TASK-013 (the in-app docs link — actual code).

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no data model changes. The only code constant introduced is:

- `WAVE_DOCS_URL` (string) — canonical docs URL, default `https://github.com/abranjith/wave-client/tree/main/docs`. Single source of truth in `packages/core`.

## 4. Files

### New — documentation (`docs/`)

- `docs/README.md` — index/landing: overview, high-level feature summary, full table of contents linking every doc
- `docs/getting-started/installation.md` — prerequisites + install, split sections for VS Code and Web app
- `docs/getting-started/quick-start.md` — first-request walkthrough with screenshot placeholders
- `docs/features/requests.md` — request types (HTTP / WebSocket / SSE), URL, headers, params, body types, response viewer, "Sent" view
- `docs/features/collections.md` — collection structure, folders, import/export (Postman/OpenAPI/HTTP), run, move/duplicate
- `docs/features/environments.md` — environment variables, global vs environment scope, selection
- `docs/features/variables.md` — `{{var}}` resolution and dynamic function variables (`_fn_`)
- `docs/features/auth.md` — auth types (API key, Bearer, Basic, OAuth); linked from requests
- `docs/features/validations.md` — validation rules, operators, JSONPath/JSON Schema
- `docs/features/wave-store.md` — Wave Store: cookies, auth, proxy, cert stores
- `docs/features/flows.md` — flow editor, aliases, JSONPath references, execution
- `docs/features/tests.md` — Test Lab: suites, assertions, runs
- `docs/features/reporting.md` — run reports (collection/flow/test), HTML export
- `docs/features/settings.md` — settings wizard, data storage, encryption
- `docs/features/ai-arena.md` — Wave Arena (Learn/Discover agents, commands) + MCP server (brief)
- `docs/platforms/vscode.md` — VS Code specifics (install, open, keybinding, storage, theme)
- `docs/platforms/web-app.md` — web app + local server (run, ports, troubleshooting)
- `docs/design.md` — architecture/design decisions: adapter pattern, package split & responsibilities, services pattern, store (Zustand) pattern, hooks usage, Result pattern (absorbs `docs/adapter-guide.md`)
- `docs/release-notes.md` — beta release highlights
- `docs/images/SCREENSHOTS.md` — capture checklist of every referenced image

### New — code

- `packages/core/src/constants/docs.ts` (or nearest existing constants location) — `WAVE_DOCS_URL`
- `packages/core/src/components/common/DocsLinkButton.tsx` — shared icon-anchor docs button
- `packages/core/src/test/components/DocsLinkButton.test.tsx` — unit tests

### Modified

- `README.md` (root) — rewrite as consolidator
- `packages/core/README.md`, `packages/vscode/README.md`, `packages/web/README.md`, `packages/server/README.md`, `packages/shared/README.md`, `packages/arena/README.md`, `packages/mcp-server/README.md` — slim to purpose-only
- `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts` — export `DocsLinkButton`
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` (if it re-exports) — export `WAVE_DOCS_URL` and `DocsLinkButton`
- `packages/web/src/components/ConfigPanel.tsx` — render `DocsLinkButton` in footer cluster
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/ConfigPanel.tsx` — render `DocsLinkButton` in footer cluster
- `.spec-lite/TODO.md` — log discovered enhancements

### Deleted (conditional)

- `docs/adapter-guide.md` — only after `design.md` verifiably covers its content (TASK-009 → TASK-014)

### Kept (explicitly NOT deleted)

- `packages/web/scripts/START_DEV_README.md`, `packages/web/src/test/README.md`, `packages/vscode/src/test/README.md`, `packages/shared/src/test/README.md`, `packages/core/src/components/testlab/README.md`, `packages/core/src/utils/functions/README.md`, `packages/mcp-server/docs/flow_feature.md` — contributor/dev docs, content not reproduced elsewhere

## 5. Dependencies

- `lucide-react` (already a dependency) for the docs icon (e.g., `BookOpenIcon`).
- No new packages. No SSG tooling added in this feature (deferred).

## 6. Implementation Tasks

> ℹ️ This is a documentation feature. For markdown-only tasks the second sub-item is a **Link & structure check** (not unit tests). TASK-013 is the only code task and uses real unit tests.
> Content for all docs is drawn from `.spec-lite/feature-summary.md` (authoritative current state); do not document features or options that do not exist there or in code.

### TASK-001: Docs scaffold, index landing, conventions & screenshot checklist

- [ ] **Content**: Create the `docs/` folder tree (`getting-started/`, `features/`, `platforms/`, `images/`). Write `docs/README.md` as the index/landing page: short product overview, high-level feature summary (grouped: Requests/Protocols, Collections, Environments & Variables, Auth, Validations, Wave Store, Flows, Test Lab, Reporting, Settings, AI/Arena), and a full table of contents linking every planned doc (use relative links; links may point at docs created in later tasks). Add a short "Documentation conventions" note (relative links, where screenshots live). Create `docs/images/SCREENSHOTS.md` with an empty-but-structured checklist (grouped by doc).
- [ ] **Link & structure check**: All TOC links use correct relative paths to the planned file locations; headings start at `#` and nest without skipping levels.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: This task *is* documentation; ensure the index explicitly tells users "start here."
- **Verify**: `docs/README.md` renders on GitHub with a working TOC; folder structure matches Section 4.

Register every image in `SCREENSHOTS.md`.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — documentation + a static external link only.
- **Error Handling**: N/A for markdown. The `DocsLinkButton` is a plain anchor (no async/error path); if `WAVE_DOCS_URL` is ever unreachable that is a browser-level concern, not app state.
- **Logging**: N/A.
- **Accessibility**: `DocsLinkButton` must have an accessible name ("Documentation") and visible tooltip, consistent with sibling footer buttons.
- **Portability**: All docs use relative links and a folder/`README.md` layout so a future SSG (VitePress/MkDocs) requires no file moves.
- **Accuracy**: All feature claims trace to `.spec-lite/feature-summary.md` or code — no aspirational/roadmap features documented as shipped (roadmap lives only in the curated "Future peek").
